---
title: "Overview and workflow"
subtitle: "The typed path from dataset to first-level fit, contrast, and group summary."
jupyter: python3
execute:
  freeze: auto
---

## The Spine

`fmrimod` runs one typed spine end to end. Every arrow below is a typed
Python value, not a string or a hand-built matrix:

```text
fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm(spec) -> contrast -> group_fit
```

This page walks the whole spine on a small synthetic example.

## Dataset

An event table plus an acquisition grid, bound together in an
`fmri_dataset`.

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import fmrimod as fm
from fmrimod.spec import hrf
from fmrimod.contrast import condition
from fmrimod.dataset import group_data_from_csv
from fmrimod.stats import group_fit, GroupFitRequest

events = pd.DataFrame({
    "onset": [0, 12, 24, 36, 48, 60],
    "stim_type": ["face", "scene"] * 3,
    "duration": 2.0,
})

dataset = fm.fmri_dataset(
    np.random.default_rng(42).normal(size=(80, 6)),
    tr=1.0,
    events=events,
)
dataset.n_timepoints, dataset.n_voxels
```

## Typed Spec → First-Level Fit

The spec is a typed `hrf(...)` value — one HRF-convolved regressor per
stimulus level. `fmri_lm` compiles it, adds the baseline, and returns an
inspectable fit.

```{python}
spec = hrf("stim_type", basis="spmg1")
fit = fm.fmri_lm(spec, dataset)

fit.betas.shape, fit.tstat().shape
```

The realized design is readable from the fit — built for you, still
inspectable.

```{python}
list(fit.model.design_matrix(run=0).columns)
```

## Typed Contrast

The scientific comparison names conditions, not column positions.

```{python}
face_vs_scene = fit.contrast(
    condition("face", term="stim_type") - condition("scene", term="stim_type"),
    name="face_vs_scene",
)
face_vs_scene.stat_type, face_vs_scene.estimate.shape, face_vs_scene.stat.shape
```

## Group Summary

Carry per-subject contrast estimates (and their standard errors) into the
typed second-level seam. `group_fit` takes a `GroupFitRequest` and returns
a typed `GroupFitResult` — closing the spine.

```{python}
rng = np.random.default_rng(0)
per_subject = pd.DataFrame([
    {"subject": f"s{i}", "roi": "face_vs_scene",
     "beta": float(rng.normal(0.3, 0.2)),
     "se": float(abs(rng.normal(0.1, 0.02)))}
    for i in range(8)
])

gd = group_data_from_csv(
    per_subject,
    effect_cols={"beta": "beta", "se": "se"},
    subject_col="subject",
    roi_col="roi",
)
group = group_fit(
    GroupFitRequest(data=gd, formula="~ 1", model="meta",
                    effects="random", tau2="pm")
)
type(group).__name__, group.estimate.round(3), group.p.round(3)
```

## Advanced Topics

The same dataset/spec contract supports typed engine options, AR
diagnostics, single-trial estimation, and benchmark fixtures. Low-level
matrix helpers exist for numerical canaries, but the flagship path stays
on the typed spine above.

## Next

- [Linear model](linear-model.qmd)
- [Contrasts](contrasts.qmd)
- [Group analysis](group-analysis.qmd)
